entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::353 - Waldo Canyon regrowth
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien and I tried to drive up to the place where we got married. We hadn't been there since that day, but in the time between then and now it was engulfed in the center of the Waldo Canyon Fire. We're interested to see what it looks like now. Unfortunately access to that area is still prohibited while the vegetation regrows.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

504qDM

entry.title: 35::296 - Ultrasound, 11 weeks
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tonight when I got off the M at West Portal there wasn't an L coming for 20 more minutes. This is why I always take any available train through the subway as far as I can. I walked for a while and found this traffic accident scene just as they were finishing the cleanup. There were give or more trains waiting to proceed.

entry.title: 35::192 - Powell Steps Bookworm
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This man frequently sits on the steps at Powell Station and reads. He must love the fact that the stairwell is closed down, because now he can read in peace without all the foot traffic on the steps.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::201 - Dave and Kynan
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::204 - Lies
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Apparently employers in California are legally required to post these lies within view of their employees. Rational citizens will recall the treatment of Edward Snowden and Bradley Manning and realize that this is really a request for good citizens to unwittingly surrender to Big Brother.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::227 - My morning chariot
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::226 - Tu Lan's Re-opening
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This diamond in the rough opened back up after a year of being closed, and ever since there's been a line out front. I managed to get in and have some of their tasty imperial rolls, and since it's right around the corner from my office I'm sure I'll be back.

Tien and I had breakfast at Millbrae Pancake House with Kyung and Kat. They showed up in their hot new ride that even has space for the family dog.

entry.title: 35::195 - Peter and Vincent ride Muni
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::182 - Vincent's 2nd day in USA
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tien's good friend and college classmate Vincent arrived in America yesterday and the head of a two year student visa and Fulbright Scholarship studying in West Virginia. He and the other Fulbright students are taking a month to study at the Monterey Institute of International Studies in Pacific Grove before heading on to their collegiate destinations. Tien and I took our third anniversary trip down to Monterey to coincide with his arrival, welcome him and see some sights together. Here they are at the Bixby Bridge.

entry.title: 35::183 - Tien at the Hearst Castle
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Three years ago Tien and I got married. Here she is at a castle.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.summary:

Raymond, Tien and I walked all the way from Japantown to Aquatic park for the fireworks, then walked all the way down to Market to catch the train home.

entry.title: 35::159 - Sand movers

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::156 - Tien in the orchard
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Today I got off work a bit late and found that the L train wasn't coming for 15 minutes. I caught the N and took it all the way to the beach. When I got off I found a beautiful sunset. With music in my ears and my camera in my hand I strolled along the path, enjoying the scene, and I found that I was not the only one.

entry.title: 35::150 - Southeastern View
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This is one of the views from my new office.

entry.title: 35::149 - Tien at MPH
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This place has basically become our church, we go there every Sunday morning.

entry.title: 35::148 - Religious extremist
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This man and his little entourage frequently stand at Powell Station by the cable car turntable and shout loudly at people about how we're all going to burn in hell. He and his crew try to coerce people to join their religious sect by vocal intimidate, taunting and condemning. I've never seen anybody say anything nice to him, and am curious if he's ever brought somebody to Jesus with his tactics.

entry.title: 35::147 - West Portal
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::145 - Sushi dinner
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

After nearly three months of trying to find a job that better suits my career goals, I signed on with Zoosk today. Tien and I went out for dinner and I told her all about it.

entry.title: 35::146 - A beautiful tree
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

To capture the whole thing I'll have to come back with something wider than 15mm.

entry.title: 35::141 - San Francisco prices in 2013
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

San Francisco has many hills and many stairways.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::142 - Opera
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::139 - Lila Tretikov, May 2013
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::138 - White rose
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::137 - Tien in dim light
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I'm amazed at the VR performance of the 70-200mm VR ii. You can shoot it hand-held at 1/(focal length/10), blowing away the traditional hand-held rule. This shot is 200mm @ 1/20s hand-held, and I can't see any motion blur when zoomed 1:1.

entry.title: 35::136 - Sandpocalypse 2013 rages on
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Several days of high wind have pushed sand out onto the great highway, closing it again. There is a roundabout and parking lot underneath all of this sand.

entry.title: 35::135 - Barn and boy
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Even after 5 years I still discover interesting locations with great views in San Francisco.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::131 - Solar Array
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::132 - Pinhole of a prism
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

A long time ago I took a Nikon body cap an drilled a big hole in it with a dremel tool. Then I cut open an aluminum can and taped part of it over the hole. Then i poked a hole in it with a sewing needle. This created a very tiny aperture. This is how you make a pinhole camera. The aperture was too small though, not enough light was coming through, and you could tell it wasn't circular. Finding something only slightly larger than a sewing needle was difficult, so today I decided to just push a thumb tack through it. This created a very nice round hole, but it was too large to make a very clear image. In any case, this is a photo I took using a piece of an aluminum can as a lens on my D800. Maybe next I'll dry tin foil, that's supposed to work well.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::127 - Crepe munching
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Last night, Tien and I went to see Star Trek 2, then we got a crepe and took some photos on our way home.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::123 - Nate at Rickhouse
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Tonight I got together with my friend Nathan for the first time in forever. We passed through Rickhouse on our way around downtown and had a sip of Ardbeg Corryvreckan. I hadn't been to Rickhouse since I had my D3s, so it was interesting to see how well the low light performance of the D800 compared. I think it did spectacularly, and although I really love the D3s, I gotta say I think the D800 is better overall.

entry.title: 35::122 - Dashboard bobble doggie
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I spent a good portion of the day driving back and forth between my house and the hospital to help my landlord with some medical errands. There is really not much to photograph around Geary and Divisadero, and I ended up hanging out in the van with this dashboard bobble doggie.

entry.title: 35::124 - Barren and windy
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This evening Aaron Poletti and I walked from my house up to the Cliff House and back, taking photos and talking.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::119 - Zapcar
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Sometimes you zip, sometimes you zap.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I'd been throwing around the idea of picking up a f/1.2 lens, but the thought of having another MF lens wasn't all that awesome. My friend Dan picked up the Canon 50mm f/1.2L AF and after looking at how great the images were I thought I'd finally pick one up, so I found this pre-AI Nikkor 55mm f/1.2 S•C on CL. It just barely fits on my D800, but I'll have to have it converted in order for it to function well. It's too bad that Canon is the only manufacturer who makes f/1.2 AF lenses.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I posted this in full resolution because the tiny details are interesting.

entry.title: 35::111 - Beach time with Bri
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Brianna just recently got back from spending over two months in SE Asia. We hung out at the beach for a few hours and she told me all about her travels.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I've been looking for work and interviewing for the last week or so, which is sometimes more stressful than it seems like it would be. Tien and I decided to go out to the old neighborhood and chow down on some delicious not so healthy food, one of our favorites, Golden Boy Pizza.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::104 - Removing sand
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The wind blew a bunch of sand onto the highway a week or so ago, so the highway has been closed down since then. The weather has been good and they finally came and cleaned the sand off, but for some reason the highway is still closed. It's nice for the pedestrians, but but the drivers take out their frustrations by driving faster than they should in a long line down our residential street.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::97 - Sunset Auto Reconstruction
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::98 - Drifting on the highway
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::96 - Studious
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::95 - Windy day on the coast
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

As I predicted, they closed down Upper Great Highway through Sunset yesterday, and the and just keeps piling up. I headed south on the coast and found this result of years of high wind.

entry.title: 35::94 - Scrapping
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I took Tien to Scrap-SF today, such a cool spot for art projects.

entry.title: 35::93 - Another victim of wind
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Another tree blown down by the recent high winds.

entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::89 - Tree down on the trail
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Last night the winds were insane, I heard that they were up to 75mph. Tien and I went running in Golden Gate Park in the morning and found this downed tree.

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

When I was young this would've been the coolest thing ever.

entry.title: 35::85 - Trouble Coffee
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::86 - Post-DMV comfort food
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

After she spent 5 hours at the DMV clearing up a mess they started nearly three years ago, Tien and I went to Japantown for sushi and sweets.

entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::78 - Carmen and Sadek, March 2013
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Today I caught up with Carmen and Sadek for the first time since their wedding, and met up with some other folks from Sugar as well.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::75 - Dropping mom and dad off at Amtrak
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This morning we got up early and dropped my parents off at the Amtrak station in San Francisco so they could head back to CO.

entry.title: 35::74 - Introducing our parents
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

For some reason it's never worked out that we were able to introduce our parents to each other, but tonight we were finally able to introduce Tien's mom to my parents via Facetime.

entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::65 - Last day at GWOS
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Today was my last day at the job I've had for about the last three years.

entry.title: 35::64 - Quintara Sunset
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

This was the result of a short video I took showing how to use the cropped shooting area of the Nikon D800.

entry.title: 35::63 - Lost in the fog
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I think this guy got lost in the fog; we never see doves up on the 16th floor, only seagulls.

entry.title: 35::62 - Zach Kurey, March 2013
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::60 - Austin-Healey Sprite
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::59 - Tien at the Stockton crossroads
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I picked up this Nikkor 28mm ƒ/2.8D lens off Craigslist today for $150 and went straight out to take pleasing photos with it. I was really happy to find an AF wide prime for a decent price. I'm a little bummed that Nikon hasn't made any cheap AF-S wide primes, their only offerings cost many hundreds of dollars and are large, heavy things. That's one reason I've been shooting the 35mm DX on my D800 so often, but now I have this even wider FX so that's rad.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::57 - Transbay Terminal dig
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::56 - Powell Station at night
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::55 - Infinity Bliss
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

The D800 has an in-cam multiple exposure feature. You can do anywhere from two to ten exposures with the same settings. This photo was taken with three exposures at 1 second each. For the first two I focused on the Infinity Towers, then took the camera and moved it sinuously upwards. The third exposure is a stationary, defocused shot of the Infinities.

entry.title: 35::39 - Mt. Tam group jump

entry.title: 35::38 - Brianna Quintero, February 2013
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

Brianna was having a pre-birthday bash tonight and I didn't realize until I got there that two days from now she is leaving for a trip to SE Asia! So exciting, I'm looking forward to seeing what she does in one of my favorite regions.

entry.title: 35::37 - Some kind of emergency
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I was drinking a jar of wine at the beach when a bunch of emergency responders drove by and stopped down at Taraval. A while later I drove by and stopped to see what was up. I didn't get the full story but heard it was something involving a surfer, and the situation had been resolved.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::32 - More tiled steps coming right up
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::31 - Our new ride
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

After two years of not owning a car Tien and I felt it was time to get back on the road. She has a goal of learning how to drive a car this year, and I am ready to start exploring more of California and resume getting out of the city on the weekends. The used car scene on CL has been brutal lately, so we caved and went to a used car dealer that had this 2003 Corolla in really good shape.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::29 - Me and the photo bomber
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::28 - D5200
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I got my D800 back today after having it repaired for drop damage that caused it to back-focus. I took this test shot of the D5200 I've been happy with while the D800 was in the shop and was pleased to see it's back to sharp focus.

D5200 is a really great camera. I highly recommend it for people who want an SLR without going too high on price.

entry.title: 35::16 - Bay Lights on display
entry.summary:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

As I was leaving work I spotted the Bay Lights on display. I went down to the water front with a beer and a cup of chili and watched for a while. Some other photographers were there and Phil McGrew explained that they were doing a test but it wasn't permanently on display yet.

I put some video of the Bay Lights on Youtube.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::14 - Sacramento Street
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::11 - Raymond, Tien and Olive
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

There's a speed boat behind that wave, but you can't see it because the wave is so large.

I got my D5200 today and this was one of the 19th photo I took with it. I'm already enjoying it as much as any other camera with the flip-out LCD form factor, but it has a feature I've long wanted and never seen on any camera I've owned: an option to automatically set the minimum shutter speed based on focal length when shooting with auto-ISO. This feature was available in the T3i, but at the expense of manual minimum shutter speed selection. The D5200 has both, and that is a beautiful thing. (I later found that the D800 also has this feature but I'd missed it.) This image was taken with that feature enabled. The camera wouldn't let the shutter time go below 1/500s with such a long zoom on a cropped sensor, so it upped the ISO to 250 to keep the shutter time fast. I didn't have to choose 1/500s, the camera selected that based on my focal length.

On the down side, Nikon really needs to implement focus peaking like the Sony NEX has. Live-view benefits so much from focus peaking, especially with the flip out screen where you're doing weird angles and need visual feedback of precise focus, eg: ground-level macros. They should be doing everything they can to get whatever legal rights they need to get in order to have that feature.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::8 - Mavericks is on
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

My grandmother passed away this morning. She was an amazing woman who lived a long, full life.
WarzauWynn posted a photo:

entry.title: 35::2 - Facetime with Ben and Thu
entry.summary: WarzauWynn posted a photo:

WarzauWynn posted a photo:

I'm 35 today. This is the beginning of my fourth 365 project.

If you're considering starting a 365 project, do it. It is very personally satisfying and much easier than writing in a journal. I treat mine as a documentary so there is no pressure to be creative on days when I am in a funk. I wish I had started years earlier.
